what: First live validation of the capability probe (shard/probe.py, PR #65/#66): full measured cap vector -> role on real hardware. GPU half on a rented 24GB 4090 (marlin path); network half cross-WAN against a 3x5090 EU pool (CH/NO/DK) with --serve peers on 29600 and dial-back through the vast port mapping (--dialback-advertise).
spec_revisions_this_forces: marlin load-peak-extra is MEASURED 4824 MB — bigger than the cutlass ~4300 assumption and far above the ~900 guess; a 24GB marlin card holds 4 layers, not the spec table's 5 (N=16 hops, not 13). The 'never anchors' verdict gets STRONGER.
marlin footprint 4057.5 MB/layer measured (spec said 4250; #61 measured 4080 MoE-only — consistent).
marlin under CUDA graph is BIT-EXACT vs eager on this box (max_abs_diff 0.0) — the fast-kernel gate could be tightened from cosine to bit-equality if this holds across arches.

rtt_to_pool is the binding constraint in practice: this 'NL' box is really ~72ms from the EU pool (geo claims are worthless, the probe catches it). At 72ms even batched B=4 misses the 20 agg bar (13.3) — batched viability is POOL-RELATIVE, the ~36 agg spec claim assumes ~19-30ms placement.
receiver-timed single-TCP-stream uplink (209.77) is ~6x below the vast listing (1316.5) — uplink thresholds should be read as single-stream receiver-timed numbers.
notes: The dtype fix (fp32 gate.weight -> bf16 cast) and the vllm-config-context GC fix were found by this run — the probe's first two live wrinkles. Probe --serve DoS caps (slowloris wall-clock, 64-conn semaphore) untested under load; fine for pool-internal use.
